title: Datadog Cloud-SIEM detection-rule inventory
description: One record per Datadog Cloud-SIEM / Security-Monitoring detection rule: the rule's name, detection class (log / signal_correlation / threshold), enabled state, highest case severity, and the NAMES/HANDLES of the notification targets it routes to. Pulled read-only from the Datadog Security-Monitoring API (GET /api/v2/security_monitoring/rules, security_monitoring_rules_read scope). This is the slice-533 sibling of monitoring.alert_config.v1 (slice 488 decision D1): a detection rule carries a severity + a detection-class field the operational alert-config shape lacks, so it gets its own kind rather than widening the shared monitoring kind into a lowest-common-denominator blob. Rule CONFIGURATION only — never firing signals, the detection query's raw log samples, matched-event payloads, the secret webhook URL behind a notification, an integration token, a recipient email address (PII), or the raw detection query. The connector reports a descriptive enabled/severity posture; the platform evaluator owns the final pass/fail per (control, scope).

rule_id.description: Datadog-native stable identifier of the detection rule (non-secret; an opaque ID, not a credential).

rule_name.description: Human-readable name of the detection rule.

detection_class.description: The rule's detection query class: 'log' (log detection), 'signal_correlation' (correlates existing signals), or 'threshold' (threshold/anomaly/impossible-travel/new-value). Descriptive string, not strictly enumerated, so a new Datadog rule kind does not require a schema bump.

enabled.description: Whether the detection rule is currently active (not disabled).

severity.description: The highest case severity the rule can produce (info / low / medium / high / critical). A descriptive label of the rule's configured severity, NEVER the content of a firing signal.

notification_targets.description: The notification targets the rule routes to — NAMES / HANDLES only, never secret webhook URLs, integration tokens, or recipient email addresses.

target_kind.description: Kind of notification target derived from the handle prefix (e.g. 'slack', 'pagerduty', 'webhook', 'opsgenie'). The KIND, not the secret.

target_name.description: The handle / channel name of the target (e.g. 'slack-sec-oncall', 'pagerduty-primary'). NEVER the secret webhook URL, integration token, or a recipient email address.
